note: Pre-generated narrative cache so the dashboard delivers high-quality per-scenario commentary without requiring any LLM API key. Regenerate with `python -m scripts.generate_insights` if you have a Groq or Anthropic key.
executive_summary:
## Executive Summary

**Claude Mythos Preview (April 7, 2026)** scored 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified — a 21 pp jump from Claude Opus 4.6 (72.5%) in roughly 14 months. Mythos autonomously discovered thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities including a 17-year-old FreeBSD RCE, signalling that autonomous AI software-engineering capability is now squarely in the *expert human* range.

**What the model says.** A sigmoid fit to all public SWE-bench scores places the inflection year — the steepest moment of capability growth — between **2024 and 2026**, depending on the scenario. From there, the labour-market projections fan out across four scenarios with Monte Carlo uncertainty (n = 5,000 per scenario, sampled from the parameter covariance of the sigmoid fit *and* truncated-normal sector-risk distributions calibrated to McKinsey, WEF, and OECD).

**Headline numbers.**
- **Optimistic** (managed transition): unemployment peaks ~12–18%.
- **Base** (no major intervention): peaks ~28–38%.
- **Pessimistic** (structural collapse): peaks ~45–58%.
- **Mythos-Accelerated** (cybersecurity cascade): peaks ~35–52%, with crisis dynamics arriving as early as 2027.

**Predictive credibility.** Leave-last-out temporal cross-validation on the SWE-bench data shows the model fitted *before* April 2026 placed Mythos within its 95% confidence interval — i.e. the curve is honestly predictive, not merely a smooth line through known points. See [`notebooks/05_temporal_validation.ipynb`](notebooks/05_temporal_validation.ipynb).

**What this is, and is not.** This is a *what-if* model. It does not forecast the future the way a weather model forecasts tomorrow's rain — the future of AI capability is reflexive, shaped by the very policy responses these projections might inform. What the model does provide is an honest, fully reproducible accounting of where the data points so far. Every parameter is fit, every uncertainty is propagated, and every artefact is logged via MLflow.

base.headline: Base Case — No Major Intervention
base.narrative:
Under the **Base Case**, AI capability follows the calibrated SWE-bench sigmoid with no policy course-correction. Unemployment median rises through the **late 2020s**, hits the 10% threshold around 2029–2031, and peaks in the **28–38%** band by ~2040 (P25–P75 Monte Carlo).

**Drivers.** Software-engineering automation drags the IT & Communications sector first (Mythos-style cascade), followed by administrative services and customer-facing roles. Sector substitution is partial — about 5% of at-risk jobs are saved by ad-hoc reskilling, and new-job creation contributes only ~0.1% of the 2025 base annually starting in 2030.

**What it would take to avoid this.** A factor-of-5 increase in the new-jobs rate (i.e. closer to the Optimistic scenario's 0.6% / yr) and aggressive reskilling lifting mitigation from 5% to 30%+ would shave roughly 15 pp off the peak.
base.policy_lever: Reskilling pipelines + new-industry investment must move from rhetoric to **funded, scaled programmes** by 2027 to bend the curve.

optimistic.headline: Optimistic — Managed Transition
optimistic.narrative:
Under the **Optimistic** scenario, governments and large employers treat AI displacement as a known shock and act accordingly. Reskilling captures **50%** of at-risk jobs; new industries (AI safety, climate tech, the care economy) absorb displaced workers at **0.6% of the 2025 base annually**, starting in 2028.

The AI capability curve is also assumed to be slightly *delayed* (inflection +1 year vs. Base) because regulatory oversight modestly slows deployment in safety-critical sectors. Median unemployment stays under 18%, with the P75 band capped near 25%.

**Historical analogy.** Closest match is the post-WWII labour reorganisation — large absolute disruption absorbed by deliberate institutional investment. This requires **political will at scale**, not technology alone.
optimistic.policy_lever: Treat AI workforce transition with the same urgency, funding, and timeline that the 2008 financial crisis received.

pessimistic.headline: Pessimistic — Structural Collapse
pessimistic.narrative:
Under the **Pessimistic** scenario, AI deployment outpaces every adaptation mechanism. The capability curve shifts **2 years earlier** (Mythos-level generalises faster than expected), reskilling captures only 2% of at-risk jobs, and new-job creation is essentially flat (~0.02% / yr starting only in 2035).

Median unemployment crosses the **30% line by ~2034**, with the P75 band exceeding **55%** by 2040. UBI/safety-net debates arrive too late to matter — by the time policy moves, the labour market has structurally re-formed around a much smaller share of compensated work.

**This scenario is not a forecast.** It is a *failure mode*: the trajectory observed when the market is allowed to clear without intervention. Its value is calibrating how much the Optimistic policy interventions are worth.
pessimistic.policy_lever: Default-state outcome if reskilling and new-industry investment stay at current (pre-Mythos) funding levels.

mythos_accelerated.headline: Mythos-Accelerated — Cybersecurity Cascade
mythos_accelerated.narrative:
Under the **Mythos-Accelerated** scenario, the cybersecurity demonstration generalises. Mythos showed autonomous discovery of zero-days at scale — the model assumes that within ~12 months this capability propagates to law, finance, and adjacent analytical knowledge work.

The AI capability curve shifts **3 years earlier** (inflection ~2031), and the IT & Communications sector takes a +35 pp boost on direct-replacement fraction (vs. +10 pp in the Base Case). New-job creation is delayed to 2032 because the disruption hits white-collar professional roles that historically drove new industry formation.

Median unemployment crosses 20% by **2028**, peaks in the **35–52%** band by ~2032. The transition window before the inflection is *narrow* — there is no opportunity for slow-moving policy.
mythos_accelerated.policy_lever: Crisis-level response required from 2027 — equivalent to wartime production reorganisation, not annual budget cycles.
